symbol_to_branchpoint_trace_manifest.title: Symbol-to-Branchpoint Traceability with Stage97.1 Adversarial Validation Evidence
symbol_to_branchpoint_trace_manifest.principle: Connect literary logic to concrete code symbols, tests, and release gates without making GitNexus mandatory at runtime.

entries.concept: Node2 renders reader-facing surface only and cannot access raw reveal authority.
entries.rationale: This protects the user's reveal-budget and authority-boundary design from prose-layer leakage.

entries.concept: Default execution remains local-first with provider_default_calls fixed at 0.
entries.rationale: This preserves the cost-control rule while allowing optional adapters to exist.

entries.concept: GraphNexus remains the internal authority graph: CodeGraph, NarrativeGraph, and StageLineageGraph.
entries.rationale: GitNexus may enrich CodeGraph, but it must not replace the narrative and lineage authority layers.

entries.concept: BranchpointLogicGraph keeps legacy literary logic alive across staged evolution.
entries.rationale: This directly answers the user's concern that past patches can become disconnected from the current core.

entries.concept: Series, macro plot, episode, micro plot, sequence, and scene remain distinct runtime levels.
entries.rationale: This keeps the user's macro/micro story architecture from collapsing into generic plotting.

entries.concept: Claude V370 runtime muscle is absorbed under V1700 authority without copying the whole Claude architecture.
entries.rationale: Stage85 must increase traceability without weakening the Stage84 surface-runtime absorption.

entries.concept: Commercial longform release evidence remains tied to three episodes, rendered scenes, and gates.
entries.rationale: This prevents Stage85 refactoring from becoming detached from product-level evidence.

entries.concept: KoreanAntiLLMFilter removes cliche and AI-like phrasing before reader-facing evaluation.
entries.rationale: This is one of the user's most visible quality concerns: sentence surface and anti-LLM texture.

entries.concept: StyleDNA preserves writerly rhythm, genre register, and prose taste as an explicit surface module.
entries.rationale: This anchors the user's authorial-DNA concept to a runtime symbol and test.

entries.concept: ClosedLoopRenderer performs render, filter, score, validate, and trace collection as a local loop.
entries.rationale: This keeps surface prose improvement measurable without delegating default judgment to an external provider.

entries.concept: SelfLearningCollector records accepted local traces as the seed of future quality improvement.
entries.rationale: This keeps self-learning as local trace evidence, not uncontrolled model fine-tuning.

entries.concept: GitNexus provides high-resolution code impact analysis while remaining optional.
entries.rationale: This is the boundary that lets developers use GitNexus without forcing users to install it.

entries.concept: SeriesArcPlanner turns a 16-episode season arc into a verified four-act graph.
entries.rationale: This absorbs V380 SeriesArcPlanner as live V1700 code instead of leaving it as a document-only concept.

entries.concept: CausalPlotGraph stores causal, foreshadow, callback, and emotional escalation edges.
entries.rationale: This protects the user's go-like event placement idea with inspectable graph edges.

entries.concept: EpisodeRevealBudget blocks premature direct reveal while permitting controlled foreshadowing.
entries.rationale: This keeps reveal budget from collapsing over multi-episode generation.

entries.concept: CharacterKnowledgeProseBridge converts per-character knowledge status into prose-safe constraints.
entries.rationale: This prevents characters from narrating facts they do not know or facts reserved for the reader.

entries.concept: KnowledgeStatus and reveal policy are folded into a Node2 SurfaceOnlyContract before prose rendering.
entries.rationale: This connects Arc-Reveal-Knowledge absorption to the existing Node2 no-structural-authority rule.

entries.concept: V380 absorption remains local-first and does not introduce default external provider calls.
entries.rationale: This keeps the user's cost-control and provider-zero operating-system principle intact.

entries.concept: Stage87 proves the minimum 8-episode scale-up evidence target with 80 scene-level contracts.
entries.rationale: This is the first explicit evidence layer beyond the Stage83 three-episode pack.

entries.concept: Stage87 proves full 16-episode season evidence with 160 scene-level contracts.
entries.rationale: This turns Stage86 Arc-Reveal-Knowledge contracts into scale evidence rather than a single smoke proof.

entries.concept: EpisodeRevealBudget and CharacterKnowledgeProseBridge remain active across long episode maps.
entries.rationale: This blocks the common regression where scale-up silently drops reveal and knowledge constraints.

entries.concept: Stage87 inherits Stage86 and Stage85 gates before accepting scale-up evidence.
entries.rationale: This keeps scale-up from becoming a parallel fork outside the branchpoint-governed OS.

entries.concept: Stage88 replaces external human editor benchmarking with a deterministic local AI-agent editor panel.
entries.rationale: This preserves the user's request to substitute external editors/readers with artificial intelligence agents while keeping the benchmark reproducible.

entries.concept: Stage88 evaluates blinded Stage87 scale-up samples without exposing model/stage identity to reviewer agents.
entries.rationale: This prevents agent scoring from becoming a self-confirming non-blind internal smoke test.

entries.concept: Stage88 release requires agent consensus, minimum agent average, and minimum sample average above the quality floor.
entries.rationale: This turns synthetic reviewer-agent output into an actual blocking release condition rather than a narrative report.

entries.concept: AI-agent benchmark runs locally and preserves provider_default_calls = 0 and Node2 raw reveal access = 0.
entries.rationale: This keeps AI-agent evaluation inside the V1700 local-first operating boundary.

entries.concept: Stage89 exposes writer-facing Studio panels while preserving V1700 branchpoint governance.
entries.rationale: This turns the engine into a writer-facing Studio contract without bypassing branchpoint survival gates.

entries.concept: Stage89 exports Studio state to JSON, Markdown, HTML, platform pack, and scene CSV artifacts.
entries.rationale: This makes export a blocking, checksum-bearing pipeline rather than an ad hoc packaging step.

entries.concept: Stage89 renders a static Writer Studio HTML preview for developer and writer handoff.
entries.rationale: This gives the project a lightweight UI artifact without introducing a web runtime dependency.

entries.concept: Writer Studio and exports preserve provider_default_calls = 0 and Node2 raw reveal access = 0.
entries.rationale: This keeps the writer-facing layer inside the same local-first boundary as Stages 84-88.

entries.concept: Stage90 applies writer-side Studio edit patches and proves they survive re-export.
entries.rationale: This makes writer edits a tested round-trip contract rather than a one-way static export.

entries.concept: Stage90 compares before/after checksums across JSON, Markdown, HTML, platform pack, and scene CSV exports.
entries.rationale: This turns export fidelity into a blocking release criterion with deterministic evidence.

entries.concept: Studio interaction and export fidelity hardening preserve provider_default_calls = 0 and Node2 raw reveal access = 0.
entries.rationale: This protects the local-first and Node2 surface-only invariants while adding writer interaction semantics.

entries.concept: Stage91 persists Writer Studio workspace snapshots with deterministic checksums and no database/provider dependency.
entries.rationale: This turns Writer Studio state into an auditable persistence contract without introducing a server runtime.

entries.concept: Stage91 routes Studio changes through a deterministic review queue with branchpoint-impact references.
entries.rationale: This protects the user's branchpoint-governed development principle when UI edits are introduced.

entries.concept: Stage91 replays Studio UI events deterministically and verifies replay checksum stability.
entries.rationale: This gives the Studio an interaction audit trail before adding a live UI server.

entries.concept: Stage91 interaction replay preserves provider_default_calls = 0 and Node2 raw reveal access = 0.
entries.rationale: This ensures Studio interactivity does not regress the local-first and Node2 surface-only invariants.

entries.concept: Stage92 configures local developer adapters for Ollama, GPT, Claude, and Gemini without making any provider call during release gates.
entries.rationale: This satisfies the developer requirement that the personal local computer can configure Ollama, GPT, Claude, and Gemini as a multi-adapter runtime.

entries.concept: Stage92 adapters remain dry-run by default and release gates never perform live provider calls.
entries.rationale: This preserves provider_default_calls = 0 while still allowing explicit developer-side provider configuration.

entries.concept: Stage92 exposes the four-provider adapter configuration through a Writer Studio panel without granting Node2 raw reveal access.
entries.rationale: This connects the adapter runtime to the Stage89-91 Studio layer while keeping the interaction and review queue lineage alive.

entries.concept: Stage93 adds a live-provider opt-in sandbox boundary for Ollama, GPT, Claude, and Gemini while release probes perform zero live calls.
entries.rationale: This lets the developer test local providers intentionally without weakening provider-zero release execution.

entries.concept: Stage93 audits provider credentials through environment presence and redacted fingerprints only.
entries.rationale: This prevents API keys from leaking into release evidence, Studio exports, or benchmark reports.

entries.concept: Stage93 normalizes Ollama, GPT, Claude, and Gemini responses into one V1700 response contract.
entries.rationale: This avoids provider-specific response shapes leaking upward into Writer Studio, Node2, or future evaluation code.

entries.concept: Stage93 release gates validate the live sandbox without executing live provider calls.
entries.rationale: This preserves provider_default_calls = 0 and Node2 raw reveal access = 0 after multi-adapter hardening.

entries.concept: Stage94 evaluates Ollama, GPT, Claude, and Gemini through a common dry-run prompt suite.
entries.rationale: This creates provider comparison evidence without letting any provider become the hidden runtime authority.

entries.concept: Stage94 scores normalized provider responses across latency, cost, tokens, safety, literary quality, and branchpoint compliance.
entries.rationale: This gives the developer a comparable provider scorecard before Stage95 ensemble arbitration.

entries.concept: Stage94 release evaluation performs zero live provider calls and keeps Node2 raw reveal access at zero.
entries.rationale: This preserves Stage93 provider sandbox safety while adding provider comparison.

entries.concept: Stage97.1 proves broken longform structures are blocked before Stage98 Studio workflow.
entries.rationale: This addresses the gap between normal proof success and failure-case blocking ability.

entries.concept: Stage97.1 binds Stage96 coefficient memory and local-only manuscript ingest to adversarial validation.
entries.rationale: This ensures learned local coefficients influence validation while raw manuscript text never leaves local evidence boundaries.

entries.concept: Stage97.1 release gates keep provider calls, raw manuscript provider leakage, and Node2 raw reveal access at zero.
entries.rationale: This preserves the operating-system boundary while adding adversarial failure-case validation.

entries.concept: Stage100 locks GitNexus-aware preflight, GraphNexus authority, and Branchpoint survival before RC declaration.
entries.rationale: This enforces the mandatory pre-development guide before declaring V1700 Literary OS 1.0 RC.

entries.concept: Stage100 separates prose-mode and scenario-mode evaluation authority.
entries.rationale: This prevents prose quality and scenario production metrics from being conflated.

entries.concept: Stage100 certifies provider contracts while preserving live provider call count zero in release mode.
entries.rationale: This confirms GPT, Claude, Gemini, Ollama, fixture, and mock providers remain governed by V1700 contracts.

entries.concept: Stage100 compares V430 strengths without merging V430 code into the RC.
entries.rationale: This keeps the RC stable while preserving a clear Stage101 absorption path.

entries.concept: Stage100 packaging excludes .git, .gitnexus, .venv, caches, pyc files, and non-POSIX paths.
entries.rationale: This gives developers a clean RC ZIP that can be handed off without local execution artifacts.

entries.concept: Stage101 probes V430 source availability and blocks untraced cross-lineage merges before absorption.
entries.rationale: This keeps V430 absorption contract-first and prevents untraced runtime import.

entries.concept: Stage101 adds a scenario-room contract layer without changing prose-mode authority.
entries.rationale: This gives scenario mode a production-room surface while preserving V1700 contracts.

entries.concept: Stage101 integrates scene beats, investigation/action beats, dialogue/silence cues, and prop reveal cues.
entries.rationale: This connects V430-style scenario movement to scene necessity, agency, Node2 boundaries, and reveal budgets.

entries.concept: Stage101 proves scenario-room absorption does not conflate prose and scenario evaluation.
entries.rationale: This keeps Stage100 dual-mode separation intact while promoting scenario-room contracts.

entries.concept: Stage102 runs a deterministic writer workflow trial over the Stage101 engine.
entries.rationale: This moves the project from internal architecture proof toward writer-facing workflow evidence.

entries.concept: Stage102 compares direct provider-style baselines and V1700 modes through blinded local scorecards.
entries.rationale: This verifies V1700 against GPT/Claude/Gemini/Ollama-style baselines without live provider calls.

entries.concept: Stage102 measures revision time reduction, issue reduction, plot consistency, payoff debt, and scene necessity.
entries.rationale: This prevents a good blind score from hiding poor writer revision economics.

entries.concept: Stage102 release evidence preserves provider-zero execution, Node2 boundary, and raw manuscript privacy.
entries.rationale: This makes Stage102 a safe evidence layer rather than an uncontrolled external benchmark.

entries.concept: Stage103 proves fresh-clone install and CI replay commands are explicit developer contracts.
entries.rationale: This moves the project from internal validation toward reproducible developer setup.

entries.concept: Stage103 separates dev, release, and sandbox runtime profiles with live providers disabled by default.
entries.rationale: This prevents production readiness work from weakening provider-zero release mode.

entries.concept: Stage103 adds local-only manuscript vault and feature-only project backup/restore probes.
entries.rationale: This keeps user manuscript privacy intact while adding operational backup discipline.

entries.concept: Stage103 standardizes safe error reports and release notes for developer handoff.
entries.rationale: This is the handoff layer that makes deployment issues reportable without leaking prompts or credentials.

entries.concept: Stage103 deployment readiness preserves provider-zero, Node2 boundary, raw manuscript privacy, and clean packaging.
entries.rationale: This ensures production hardening does not become an uncontrolled provider or packaging regression.
